t_ch: 您果然是一位充满好奇心、观察力敏锐、极具谋略与智慧的选手！
t_en: You're indeed a contestant who has keen curiosity, astute powers of observation, and is full of tactics and intellect!

t_ch: 您果然是一位充满好奇心、观察力敏锐、极具谋略与智慧的选手！
t_en: You're indeed a contestant who has keen curiosity, astute powers of observation, and is full of tactics and intellect!

t_ch: 您果然是一位充满好奇心、观察力敏锐、极具谋略与智慧的选手！
t_en: You're indeed a contestant who has keen curiosity, astute powers of observation, and is full of tactics and intellect!

t_ch: 您果然是一位充满好奇心、观察力敏锐、极具谋略与智慧的选手！
t_en: You're indeed a contestant who has keen curiosity, astute powers of observation, and is full of tactics and intellect!

t_ch: 有人来寻宝吗…
t_en: Any treasure hunters here...?

t_ch: 亲爱的挑战者，你正在前往一个隐蔽的战场——
t_en: Dear challenger, you're about to head to a hidden battlefield —

t_ch: 在美梦小镇的边疆，钟表小子与镇民们都看不到的地方，哈努兄弟从未停止他的战斗。
t_en: Brother Hanu never ceased his fight at the frontier of Dreamville, where neither Clockie nor the residents can see him.

t_ch: 你是否有勇气与他面对源源不断的强敌，经受住《哈努战线》的挑战呢？
t_en: Do you have what it takes to face endless powerful enemies with him and withstand the challenge of Hanu Wars?

t_ch: 你们休想夺走钟表小子！
t_en: You're not taking Clockie away!

t_ch: 《钟表小子》永远属于克劳克影业，属于「钟表匠」先生！你们家族休想夺走它！
t_en: Clockie will always belong to Clock Studios and Mr. Watchmaker! You Family people can never have it!

t_ch: 先生，别这么激动，我是代表鸢尾花家系来帮你们的。
t_en: Sir, please calm down. I'm here to help you on behalf of the Iris Family.

t_ch: 钟表小子——这个可爱的小家伙为匹诺康尼带来了无数的财富，如今他陷入困境，人美心善的「音符小姐」自然会伸出援手。
t_en: The lovely Clockie has brought immense wealth to Penacony. Now that he's in a bind, the kind and beautiful Miss Note is ready to lend a hand.

t_ch: 抱歉，钟表小子没有什么困境，也不需要这种虚伪的善意！
t_en: I'm sorry, but Clockie isn't in any trouble, nor does he need such hypocritical goodwill!

t_ch: 是吗？但你们的财务报告里可不是这么说的啊——
t_en: Are you sure? The financial report of your company seems to tell a different tale though...

t_ch: 形象专利即将过期、{RUBY_B#「钟表匠」}董事长{RUBY_E#}失踪、纪念影片跳票…现在让发生事故的影城紧急开业，也是顶不住资金压力了吧？
t_en: The patent is about to expire, {RUBY_B#Watchmaker}the chairman{RUBY_E#} has gone missing, and the commemorative movie has been postponed... The urgent reopening of the theme park despite the accident is your way to ease the cash flow pressure, right?
Umm...

t_ch: 不靠家族，贵影业该如何解决这些难题呢？难道在银幕外有人能用「钟表把戏」来帮你们吗？
t_en: How is Clock Studios going to solve all these problems without The Family's help? Is there anyone outside the silver screen that can save the company with "clockwork"?

t_ch: 你别说，还真有。
t_en: Believe it or not, there actually is.

t_ch: 谁叫我？我来了！
t_en: Who called upon me? I have arrived!

t_ch: 不好意思，游客，我们正在讨论商务事宜，请您稍等……
t_en: I'm sorry, guest. We're in the middle of a business discussion here. Please bear with us...

t_ch: 等等，就让宾客给你最真实的反馈吧！
t_en: Wait, why don't you let the guest give you the most genuine feedback?

t_ch: 看看现在的克劳克影视乐园——最具有标志性的银屏遭到破坏，天空散发着阴森的气息，惊梦剧团四处横行。
t_en: Just look at Clock Studios Theme Park right now — The most iconic silver screen is broken, the sky is all gloomy, and the Dreamjolt Troupe is walking all over the place.

t_ch: 确实有点不适合。
t_en: Not really.

t_ch: 你看，这可是宾客的观点，客观真实。
t_en: Look, that's the guest's objective and genuine opinion.

t_ch: 多刺激啊，就好这口儿。
t_en: How thrilling, just the way I like it.

t_ch: 这…《钟表小子》居然让人感到的不是温馨快乐，而是惊险与刺激，这不是什么好事。
t_en: Well... Can't believe Clockie is now being associated with excitement and thrills instead of warmth and joy... This doesn't bode well.

t_ch: 还是朝露公馆更好玩。
t_en: Dewlight Pavilion is more fun.

t_ch: 唉，作为乐园，如今的魅力还不如我们的办公机构——
t_en: Alas, it's a shame that the theme park has even less pull than our office now...

t_ch: …但我还是要提醒你一下，朝露公馆可不能随便进出。
t_en: ...Anyway, I must remind you that Dewlight Pavilion isn't somewhere you can come and go at will.

t_ch: 受众的反馈已经给出了答案——你们无用的挣扎只是在搞砸自己的口碑。
t_en: Now, your audience's feedback is your answer — you are shooting yourselves in the foot with your futile effort to turn things around.

t_ch: 就、就算这样，我们也绝不会出卖《钟表小子》的，请你离开吧！
t_en: E—Even so, we'll never sell Clockie. Please see yourself out!

t_ch: 行吧，你早晚会想通的。容我多嘴一句——等到贵影业向苜蓿草家系申请破产的时候，你就再也见不到这么丰厚的诚意了。
t_en: Alright, I'm sure you'll come to your senses sooner or later. However, there might no longer be such a generous offer on the table by the time Clock Studios files for bankruptcy with the Alfalfa Family.

t_ch: 可恶……
t_en: Darn it...

t_ch: 不用着急，好好考虑一下，我先告辞了。
t_en: There's no rush, so I suggest that you sleep on my offer. I'll take my leave now.

t_ch: 哦，该死的鸢尾花，这下我们该怎么办啊？
t_en: Oh, that blasted Iris Family! What should we do now?

t_ch: 咕噜噜，看来只能靠《美梦往事》了！
t_en: *gurgle* Seems like we can only pin our hopes on Dreamjoy Memoir now!

t_ch: 哼，但是「钟表匠」先生……
t_en: Hmm, but Mr. Watchmaker...

t_ch: 行了，还有宾客在这呢，别让人家看笑话了，快点去工作吧！
t_en: Well, let's not bother our guest with our problems. Get back to work!

t_ch: 而克劳克影业却连最基本的修复资金都拿不出来，还急着想要开张营业，真是可笑……
t_en: Clock Studios can't even dish out enough funds to fix the place, yet they want to open the doors and start operating ASAP. How ridiculous...

t_ch: 游客，你说一个充满危机、死气沉沉、破破烂烂的影视乐园，真的适合作为消费娱乐的景点吗？
t_en: Hey, guest, let's hear your take. Do you think this dangerous, lifeless, and ill-maintained theme park can be a good recreational spot?

t_ch: 抱歉，宾客，刚刚发生了一些…小小的商务纠纷，让您见笑了。
t_en: That was just a... minor business dispute. I'm sorry you had to see it.

t_ch: 我叫皮斯，是钟表小子系列动画的「制片人」，欢迎您来到克劳克影视乐园！
t_en: My name is Pisce, the producer for the Clockie franchise. Welcome to the Clock Studios Theme Park!

t_ch: 你怎么打扮成这样？
t_en: Why are you dressed like this?

t_ch: 怎么样，很有趣吧？滴答，像不像匹诺康尼的卡通明星——钟表小子？
t_en: This is fun, isn't it? Tick-tock, do I look like the cartoon star of Penacony — Clockie?

t_ch: 你长得好眼熟。
t_en: You look familiar.

t_ch: 哈哈，这是匹诺康尼的卡通明星——钟表小子！
t_en: Haha, this is the cartoon star of Penacony — Clockie!

t_ch: 我去！钟表小子！
t_en: Holy Stellaroni! Clockie!?

t_ch: 没错，就是钟表小子，匹诺康尼的吉祥物，美梦小镇的大明星！
t_en: That's right! It's Clockie — the mascot of Penacony and the brightest star in Dreamville!

t_ch: 最近克劳克影城重新营业，我们剧组成员就都打扮成了《钟表小子》里的角色，用更亲切的方式与观众交流。
t_en: All our cast members are dressing up as the characters in Clockie for the recent reopening of the theme park. We want to look more approachable while interacting with the audience.

t_ch: 对了，您不要太在意刚刚那位女士的话，我们只是碰上了一点…小小的经费问题，克劳克影业不会这么轻易就被打垮的——呃，应该不会。
t_en: By the way, please don't mind what the lady said just now. We're just having a... minor budget issue. Clock Studios will never go under that easily... I think.

t_ch: 出了什么问题？
t_en: What kind of issue?

t_ch: 这个嘛…您应该也知道，克劳克影业是那位神秘的「钟表匠」创办的企业。
t_en: Well... As you know, Clock Studios was established by the mysterious Watchmaker.

t_ch: 这位大亨一直在幕后指导影业的发展。多亏了他的商业智慧与天马行空的创意，《钟表小子》才能有今天。
t_en: The tycoon has been at the helm of the company's development behind the scenes. Clockie only gets to see its success today thanks to his business acumen and wild imagination.

t_ch: 但前一阵子，我们与他失去了联络，影业变得群龙无首，在市场竞争中屡屡受挫……
t_en: Yet, we lost touch with him a while back, and the company's been a rudderless drift since then. We keep losing ground in the market competition as a result...

t_ch: 家族想要干什么？
t_en: What's The Family after?

t_ch: 他们的代表跑来提议收购钟表小子的版权，自称这是在拯救克劳克影业——
t_en: Their representative made us an offer to buy the copyrights of Clockie, claiming that they were trying to save the company...

t_ch: 呸！真是一派胡言，没有了钟表小子的克劳克影业还有什么存在的意义！
t_en: Ugh! That's just baloney! There's no point in keeping Clock Studios if it means losing Clockie!

t_ch: 有什么解决方法吗？
t_en: Is there a solution to fix this?

t_ch: 办法倒是有…克劳克影业一直在筹备钟表小子五百周年的纪念作品——<color=#dbc291ff>《美梦往事》系列影片！</color>
t_en: There is... We've been preparing a film series to commemorate the 500th anniversary of Clockie called Dreamjoy Memoir!

t_ch: 这是观众们期待已久的作品，也是《钟表小子》开播数百年来的故事集合。<color=#dbc291ff>如果成功上映，影业必能依靠票房收入一转颓势，修复影视乐园的资金也能得到解决。</color>
t_en: It's a much-anticipated piece that is a collection of all Clockie stories over the past five centuries. If we get to release the series, I believe we can turn the tide with the box office earnings and fund the refurbishment of the theme park.

t_ch: 但不幸的是…这部影片的胶卷在之前的事故中受到严重破坏。我们竭力修复也只能还原出一些混乱的片段，还混杂着废案的剧情，<color=#dbc291ff>必须重新进行剪辑。</color>
t_en: The thing is, the film reels of the series were severely damaged in the earlier accident. Despite our best efforts to repair them, we can only salvage some fragmented scenes, mixed with scrapped storylines. <color=#dbc291ff>We have to re-edit it.</color>

t_ch: 唉，谁能料到担任导演的「钟表匠」会不知所踪呢？剧组的成员都知道，钟表小子的故事灵感来源于他的经历——但我们没人了解他的背景，也不敢擅自接手导演的工作。
t_en: Alas, who would have seen the Watchmaker's disappearance coming? He's the director of the film, and every crew member knows that Clockie's story is inspired by his experiences. However, none of us knows much about his background, nor do we dare to take on the director's role without authorization.

t_ch: 为了钟表小子的未来，如果您有「钟表匠」的消息或<color=#dbc291ff>了解「钟表匠」故事的人</color>，请一定要告知我们！
t_en: For Clockie's future, please keep us posted if you know anything about the Watchmaker's whereabouts or someone who's <color=#dbc291ff>familiar with his story</color>!

t_ch: 我了解「钟表匠」的故事。
t_en: I'm familiar with the Watchmaker's story.

t_ch: 真的吗？可不要在这种时候跟我开玩笑啊！
t_en: Really? Now isn't the time to joke around!

t_ch: 真的，不骗你。
t_en: I'm telling the truth.

t_ch: 这个…宾客，我不是不想相信您，但梦中有太多虚假的事——口说无凭，请拿出一些证据吧。
t_en: Well... It's not that I find you untrustworthy, but there are simply too many things that are untrue in dreams. I'd appreciate it if you could show me some proof.

t_ch: （出示「钟表匠」的邀请函）
t_en: (Present the invitation letter from the Watchmaker)

t_ch: 原来您是「钟表匠」先生的客人，失礼了…但这只能证明董事长邀请您来到了这里，没办法证明您了解他啊。
t_en: I see you're Mr. Watchmaker's guest. Please forgive my rudeness... Still, it only proves that Mr. Watchmaker invited you here. It doesn't mean you know him well.

t_ch: （出示「钟表匠」的礼帽）
t_en: (Present the Watchmaker's hat)

t_ch: 这是…「钟表匠」先生的帽子！
t_en: This is... Mr. Watchmaker's hat!

t_ch: 没错，绝不会认错的，他与我见面时就是戴着这顶礼帽。听说这是对他非常重要的东西，如今却在您的手中……
t_en: Yes, I'm pretty sure this is it. He was wearing this hat when I met him. I heard it's something very important to him, but now it's in your hands...

t_ch: 我明白了，尊敬的宾客，请跟我前往放映室吧——我来带你看看「钟表匠」先生留下的作品。
t_en: I've got it, my esteemed guest. Please follow me to the theater... I'll show you the work Mr. Watchmaker left behind.

t_ch: 祝你好运。
t_en: Good luck.

t_ch: 谢谢您的祝福，宾客，我们一定会尽力拯救钟表小子的！
t_en: Thanks for your wish! We'll give it our all to rescue Clockie!

t_ch: 宾客，为了钟表小子的未来，如果您有「钟表匠」的消息或<color=#dbc291ff>了解「钟表匠」故事的人</color>，请一定要告知我们！
t_en: So, dear guest, for Clockie's future, please keep us posted if you know anything about the Watchmaker's whereabouts or someone who's <color=#dbc291ff>familiar with his story</color>!

t_ch: 我了解「钟表匠」的故事。
t_en: I'm familiar with the Watchmaker's story.

t_ch: 真的吗？可不要在这种时候跟我开玩笑啊！
t_en: Really? Now isn't the time to joke around!

t_ch: 真的，不骗你。
t_en: I'm telling the truth.

t_ch: 这个…宾客，我不是不想相信您，但梦中有太多虚假的事——口说无凭，请拿出一些证据吧。
t_en: Well... It's not that I find you untrustworthy, but there are simply too many things that are untrue in dreams. I'd appreciate it if you could show me some proof.

t_ch: （出示「钟表匠」的邀请函）
t_en: (Present the invitation letter from the Watchmaker)

t_ch: 原来您是「钟表匠」先生的客人，失礼了…但这只能证明董事长邀请您来到了这里，没办法证明您了解他啊。
t_en: I see you're Mr. Watchmaker's guest. Please forgive my rudeness... Still, it only proves that Mr. Watchmaker invited you here. It doesn't mean you know him well.

t_ch: （出示「钟表匠」的礼帽）
t_en: (Present the Watchmaker's hat)

t_ch: 这是…「钟表匠」先生的帽子！
t_en: This is... Mr. Watchmaker's hat!

t_ch: 没错，绝不会认错的，他与我见面时就是戴着这顶礼帽。听说这是对他非常重要的东西，如今却在您的手中……
t_en: Yes, I'm pretty sure this is it. He was wearing this hat when I met him. I heard it's something very important to him, but now it's in your hands...

t_ch: 我明白了，尊敬的宾客，现在也许只有您能够拯救《美梦往事》系列影片了。
t_en: I understand, dear guest. You may be the only one who can save the Dreamjoy Memoir movie series now.

t_ch: 我不太了解，祝你好运。
t_en: I don't really know. Good luck.

t_ch: 谢谢您的祝福，宾客，我们一定会尽力拯救钟表小子的！
t_en: Thanks for your wish! We'll give it our all to rescue Clockie!

t_ch: 没想到您这么快就掌握了梦境动画的制作技法，而且这剪辑气质、演出风格、情节编排——简直和「钟表匠」先生的作品一模一样，太了不起了！
t_en: I'm surprised you've mastered the production techniques of Dreamscape animation so quickly! Your editing style, performance, and plot arrangement are just like Mr. Watchmaker's. You've done a terrific job!

t_ch: 宾客…不，导演，我在此代表克劳克影业，郑重邀请您加入《美梦往事》动画剧组！
t_en: Dear guest... I mean, director, I'd like to sincerely invite you on behalf of Clock Studios to join the production team of the Dreamjoy Memoir animation!

t_ch: 这就让我当导演了？
t_en: That's it? I'm the director now?

t_ch: 没错，梦境动画的后期制作并不困难，不需要高超的技术，我们缺少的是一个了解「钟表匠」、并能得到他的认可的导演——毕竟这是属于「钟表匠」的故事。
t_en: That's right! The post-production of Dreamscape animations isn't too difficult and doesn't require advanced skills. We only need a director who knows the Watchmaker well and whose ability is recognized by him. After all, this is his story.

t_ch: 我可没说我要干。
t_en: I didn't say I wanted the job.

t_ch: 拜托了！这部影片是「钟表匠」先生非常看重的作品——我们绝不能让他跟克劳克影视乐园一起在梦境中消亡！
t_en: Please do us a favor! The film series means a lot to Mr. Watchmaker! We can't allow it and the theme park to go into oblivion in the Dreamscape!

t_ch: 我的片酬可是很高的！
t_en: My services are expensive!

t_ch: 放心吧，等到这部影片成功上映，克劳克影业一定会重新崛起——到时候我们一定会拿出让您满意的报酬！
t_en: Don't worry. Clock Studios is bound to rise once this series hits the cinemas. We'll surely pay you handsomely when the time comes!

t_ch: 其他几部《美梦往事》的胶卷也会陆续完成修复，完成剪辑就能上映。
t_en: The other film reels of the Dreamjoy Memoir series will also be restored one by one. Once the editing is completed, they are ready to be released.

t_ch: 对了，刚刚的剪辑工作中，你应该得到了一份<color=#dbc291ff>「外传胶卷」</color>吧？
t_en: By the way, during the editing work just now, you should have received an <color=#dbc291ff>"Extra Story reel"</color>, no?

t_ch: 导演，我们的剪辑工作可以开始了吗？
t_en: Director, can we start editing now?

t_ch: 开始剪辑。
t_en: Begin editing.

t_ch: 我想问你点问题。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 好嘞，深入了解才能创造佳作，您尽管问，我知无不言！
t_en: Sure! An in-depth understanding of the subject is essential in creating a masterpiece. Just ask away! I'll tell you everything I know.

t_ch: 《美梦往事》讲的是什么？
t_en: What is Dreamjoy Memoir about?

t_ch: 您应该知道，「美梦小镇」就是钟表小子和他的伙伴们生活的小镇，这部系列影片就是关于美梦小镇的故事。
t_en: The series revolves around the story of Dreamville, where Clockie and his friends live — I bet you already know this.

t_ch: 钟表小子来到美梦小镇、与镇民们共甘共苦、帮助美梦小镇发展壮大…这一系列故事都会被涵盖在这套影片之中，可以说是《钟表小子》至今以来的故事总集。
t_en: The series will delve into Clockie's arrival in Dreamville, his experiences of sharing life's ups and downs with the townspeople, and his contributions to the town's growth and development... It can be seen as a comprehensive collection of Clockie's story from the very beginning.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼是怎么制作动画的？
t_en: How is animation production done in Penacony?

t_ch: 在匹诺康尼，制作动画的方式非常简单——除了智能绘制、思维显像、算法生成这种常见方法，梦中还有擅长绘画的美梦剧团，可以还原古老的手绘动画质感……
t_en: Producing animations in Penacony is pretty simple. Apart from the common techniques like smart illustration, mental projection, and algorithmic generation, there's also the Sweet Dreams Troupe which is skilled in drawing and capable of recreating the vintage hand-drawn graphic style...

t_ch: 但在克劳克影业，我们的动画分镜都是「钟表匠」先生手工绘制，他也要求我们的动画师亲手创作每一帧的内容。
t_en: But here at Clock Studios, our storyboards are all hand-drawn by Mr. Watchmaker. He insists that our animators create the content for each frame themselves by hand.

t_ch: 现在宇宙中的动画技术越来越高超，画面也越来越精细。但那些作品还是无法在梦中打败手工绘制的《钟表小子》，这也是我们剧组的骄傲！
t_en: As animation technology in the universe continues to advance, the graphics of films nowadays become increasingly sophisticated. Still, none of those works can rival the hand-drawn Clockie in Penacony. We're proud of that!

t_ch: 关于影城遭到的破坏。
t_en: About the destruction of the theme park.

t_ch: 唉，您一说这个我就郁闷，本来影城就是我们最重要的线下收入之一，但前些日子突然被猎犬家系封闭，说是要安全检查……
t_en: Oh, the mere mention of it saddens me. The theme park has always been one of our most important sources of offline revenue, but it was unexpectedly shut down by the Bloodhound Family for what they called a safety inspection...

t_ch: 结果他们检查完之后，我们观影区的大屏幕就被劈成了两半，美梦剧团也都变成了惊梦剧团。猎犬家系还推脱责任，说这是什么「外来者造成的破坏」，真是荒谬！
t_en: Guess what happened after their inspection? The screen in the theater was split in half, and the Sweet Dreams Troupe were all turned into Dreamjolt Troupe. They even tried talking their way out of it by saying the destruction was the doing of some outsiders. How ridiculous!

t_ch: 这还没完，之后隐夜鸫家系的人跑过来，说这里受到的破坏并非寻常，是什么…「虚无」的力量？反正拽了一堆名词，开了一个我们无法接受的修理费。
t_en: That's not all. Later, a bunch of Nightingale Family members showed up, asserting that the destruction was unusual and exhibited signs of some... power of Nihility? Anyway, they inundated us with a barrage of technical terms and demanded an exorbitant repair fee that we simply couldn't accept.

t_ch: 不久之后，鸢尾花家系的人就来聊版权收购——要我看啊，这就是家族内部串通好的，就是想打压我们克劳克影业，夺走钟表小子！
t_en: Shortly after, the Iris Family representative approached us and proposed to acquire our copyrights. If you ask me, those lineages are probably in cahoots with each other to take advantage of us when we're down and claim Clockie as their own!

t_ch: 关于你对「钟表匠」的印象。
t_en: About your impression of the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 这个嘛…我在克劳克影业干了大半辈子，但其实也只见过董事长一面——
t_en: Well... Although I've worked at Clock Studios for most of my life, I've only met the chairman once...

t_ch: 那是我在克劳克影业的升职面谈，他居然把面谈地点放在了「夕阳号」的游轮舞会上。
t_en: It was an interview for my promotion. He actually held the interview at a party on the cruise ship "The Sunset."

t_ch: 我当时喝得醉醺醺的，被人拽到了深处的客房，在那里看到一个头戴礼帽、身着华服的绅士坐在窗边，遥望绚烂的梦泡之海。
t_en: I already had a little too much to drink when I was dragged to a guest room deep in the corridor, inside of which was an immaculately dressed gentleman wearing a hat. He sat next to a window, staring into the distance at the colorful ocean of dream bubbles.

t_ch: 他问我：「你认为《钟表小子》能带给人们什么」，我回答：「幸福、欢笑、感动，以及对美梦的一点儿反思」——但说实话，最后一个是我喝醉了瞎掰的。
t_en: He asked, "In your opinion, what does Clockie bring to the people?" I replied, "Happiness, laughter, heartwarming moments, and a bit of reflection on the sweet dreams." Honestly, the last one was just me blabbering away in my state at the time.

t_ch: 他听到这个答案过后笑了笑，就让我离开了房间。第二天，我就收到了总制片人的任命通知…真是个神秘的人啊。
t_en: He chuckled at my answer before dismissing me from the room. The following day, I received the appointment notice from the executive producer... What a mysterious guy.

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 下次再说。
t_en: Maybe next time.

t_ch: 好吧，但时间不等人。还是希望您能尽快完成剪辑，好让我们早些度过难关。
t_en: Well, time and tide wait for no man. It'd be best if you could complete the editing as soon as possible so that we can get through this rough patch sooner.

t_ch: 在票房的资金支持下，隐夜鸫彻夜施工，「虚无」的气息逐渐从天空中消散了……
t_en: Supported by the box office's earnings, the Nightingales work through the night, and the Nihility's miasma gradually fades from the sky...

t_ch: 导演，辛苦了，这部影片已经成功上映了，市场反响非常好!
t_en: Thanks for your hard work, director! We've successfully released this film, which garners a lot of positive responses in the market!

t_ch: 多亏了票房的收益，我们修复工程也重新启动了——看，现在的影城已经好多了！
t_en: We also get to resume the refurbishment project for the theme park with the box office earnings... Look, the place is in much better shape now!

t_ch: 不过我真好奇，家族口中的「外来者」到底做了什么事情，居然让{RUBY_B#忆质}梦的本体{RUBY_E#}都瓦解了……
t_en: Still, I really wonder what on earth the "outsiders" mentioned by The Family did that left even the {RUBY_B#memoria}dream itself{RUBY_E#} crumbled...

t_ch: 是「虚无」的力量。
t_en: It was the power of Nihility.

t_ch: 真的吗？家族的人也是这么说的，我还以为他们是在忽悠我……
t_en: Really? The Family people said the same thing, but I thought they were just trying to fool me...

t_ch: 只是劈了一刀。
t_en: They only made a sword slash.

t_ch: 天呐，一刀就能把整个影城砍成这样，那这个外来者可真是恐怖……
t_en: Holy moly, the outsider must be a scary figure to cause this much damage with just one slash.

t_ch: 影城的质量真差。
t_en: The theme park is such a jerry-built project.

t_ch: 请别这么说，这里的筑梦师可不是一般人……
t_en: Please don't say that. The Dreamweavers here are all extraordinarily talented...

t_ch: 「钟表匠」说过，这里是格拉克斯教授的弟子「冯恩」设计的，质量绝对没问题。
t_en: According to the Watchmaker, this place was designed by Vaughn, a student of Professor Glaux. There shouldn't be any problem with the quality of the construction.

t_ch: 算了，不细想了，我们耗费巨资雇佣的筑梦师团队很快就会修好这里，用不了多久，您就能看到一个全新的克劳克影视乐园！
t_en: Let's not dwell on this matter anymore. Our Dreamweaver team, whom we hired with great pay, is going to spruce this place up. You'll see an all-new Clock Studios Theme Park soon!

t_ch: 对了，最近匹诺康尼商业街的客户看中了我们的影片，提议进行广告合作，这肯定能给我们带来更多收益！
t_en: By the way, some clients in Penacony's shopping district have shown interest in our film, and want to collaborate with us to produce some advertisements. This will definitely bring us more revenue!

t_ch: 导演，请先去拍摄赞助企业的<color=#dbc291ff>广告短片</color>吧，一定能学到许多「导演技巧」。
t_en: Director, please go shoot the sponsoring company's <color=#dbc291ff>commercial short</color> first, and you'll be sure to learn plenty of Directorial Skills.

t_ch: 修复工程得到了进展，影城银幕上的虚无侵蚀逐渐消退了……
t_en: The refurbishment project is progressing along, and the corrosion of Nihility on the theme park's screen gradually fades...

t_ch: 导演，我们的影片越来越火了，之前跑掉的投资人全都后悔了！
t_en: Director, our film is all the rage now! Those investors who pulled out before are regretting their decisions!

t_ch: 影城的修复也快要完成了，相信我们马上就可以在这个大荧幕上播放《美梦往事》的故事了，想想就令人高兴！
t_en: The refurbishment of the theme park is also close to completion. I believe we'll be able to play Dreamjoy Memoir on the big screen soon. I'm excited already just imagining it!

t_ch: 不过…观众的影评里除了对动画本身的褒奖，还有许多奇怪的分析与考据，猜测我们的故事是关于某种东西的寓言或隐喻……
t_en: There's one thing about the viewers' reviews though. Aside from compliments for the animation itself, there are many bizarre analyses and references speculating that our story serves as an allegory or metaphor for something...

t_ch: ——甚至有传闻说，我们的动画里藏着「钟表匠」留下的宝藏密码，太夸张了！
t_en: ...There are even rumors saying that the key to a secret treasure left behind by the Watchmaker is hidden in our animation. That's way too far-fetched!

t_ch: 是过度解读吧？
t_en: I suppose the viewers are over-interpreting the whole thing.

t_ch: 应该是吧，但我们也不能完全否认这种观点，答案可能只有「钟表匠」先生知道。
t_en: I guess so, but I can't rule out the possibility completely. Mr. Watchmaker might be the only one who knows for sure.

t_ch: 反正这些讨论也为我们的影片带来了不少热度，让观众发散一下也好。
t_en: Nonetheless, those discussions have generated a lot of buzz for our film. It's nice to see the audience having different interpretations of the story.

t_ch: 或许真的有呢？
t_en: Maybe there really is a secret treasure?

t_ch: 哈哈，是吗？可惜我学识尚浅，看不出来太多头绪。
t_en: Haha, really? I'm afraid I'm not knowledgeable enough to pick up any clues.

t_ch: 这种说法已经存在很久了。但说实话，连我们剧组自己都没法完全否定他们的观点，毕竟答案只有「钟表匠」先生知道。
t_en: The rumor has been around for a long time, but not even we know enough to disprove it. After all, only Mr. Watchmaker knows the answer.

t_ch: 宝藏密码在哪！
t_en: Where is the key to the treasure!?

t_ch: 呃…只是观众瞎猜的，您不要当真啊。
t_en: Um... It's just a wild speculation of the viewers. Please don't take it seriously.

t_ch: 但我也不能说这一定是流言——毕竟「钟表匠」先生做过太多让人难以预料的事情了。
t_en: But then again, I can't say for sure it's a mere rumor. After all, Mr. Watchmaker has done too many things that surprise us.

t_ch: 呵呵，无论如何，《美梦小镇》传奇都为我们的观众带来了欢笑与感动，也让克劳克影业脱离了苦海。
t_en: Anyway, Dreamville has brought a joyful and heartwarming experience for our viewers and helped Clock Studios out of a tight spot.

t_ch: 您知道吗？就连鸢尾花家系的「美梦剧团」都希望找我们合作。近期「惊梦剧团」制造的案件增多，他们想通过钟表小子的<unbreak>IP</unbreak>来挽救自己的形象。
t_en: You know what? Even Sweet Dreams Troupe from the Iris Family has reached out to us for collaboration. The crimes committed by Dreamjolt Troupe are on the rise lately, so they want to salvage their reputation by associating themselves with the Clockie <unbreak>franchise</unbreak>.

t_ch: 虽然之前有些矛盾，但市场中没有永远的敌人——如果您对这活儿感兴趣，也可以去处理一下，也算是个掌握「导演技巧」的好机会。
t_en: We've had some differences with them, but there are no eternal enemies in the business world. If you're interested, you can handle the collaboration. It's a good opportunity to hone your Directorial Skills.

t_ch: 加油，导演，我们很快就能让克劳克影业再次伟大了！
t_en: Keep it up, Director! I believe we can make Clock Studios great again!

t_ch: 导演，请先去拍摄美梦剧团的<color=#dbc291ff>广告短片</color>吧，一定能学到许多「导演技巧」。
t_en: Director, please go shoot the Sweet Dreams Troupe's <color=#dbc291ff>commercial short</color> first, and you'll be sure to learn plenty of Directorial Skills.

t_ch: 影片为克劳克影业带来了巨额收益，影城也逐渐恢复了原状……
t_en: The film generates a windfall for Clock Studios, and the theme park gradually regains its former status...

t_ch: 导演，您听说了吗，宇宙各大影院都挤满了《美梦往事》的观众！来克劳克影视乐园游览的宾客也越来越多了！
t_en: Director, do you know that the cinemas across the universe are thronged by viewers of Dreamjoy Memoir? Our theme park is seeing more and more guests too!

t_ch: 多少年了…自从「钟表匠」先生不知所踪之后，我们便再也没有见过这样的场面。
t_en: It's been so many years... Since we lost contact with Mr. Watchmaker, we've never seen such a bustling scene.

t_ch: 一波三折的剧情，新意迭出的桥段，生动活泼的人物…我还是第一次从那些挑剔的影评家口中听到一致的好评，是您的才华让克劳克影业起死回生！
t_en: The story is full of twists and turns, has a creative plot, and features lively characters... This has been my first time hearing nothing but good reviews from those fussy movie critics. It's your talent that brought Clock Studios back to life!

t_ch: 举手之劳而已。
t_en: It's no big deal.

t_ch: 您谦虚了，我说的绝非溢美之词。
t_en: You're being modest. Trust me, I don't mean this as flattery.

t_ch: 确实全靠我。
t_en: Indeed, I made it happen.

t_ch: 是啊，但也请不要忽视「钟表匠」先生的创意和动画师们的付出，他们也很努力！
t_en: It's true, but let's not forget about Mr. Watchmaker's creative ideas and the animators' efforts. They worked hard too!

t_ch: 接下来该干什么？
t_en: What's next?

t_ch: 这个嘛…现在无论是影评家还是普通影迷，都在期待最后一部影片——《美梦大机密》的上映。
t_en: Well... Both critics and fans are looking forward to the release of the last film — Dreamjoy Secrecy.

t_ch: 正好屏幕快修好了，可以在影视乐园重新举办这部影片的展映会，但是……
t_en: The screen should be fixed soon, just in time for the film premiere to be held at the theme park, but...

t_ch: 你们修不好胶卷了？
t_en: The film reel hasn't been fixed yet?

t_ch: 不，我们早就完成了这部胶卷的修复，但现在它并不在我们手里……
t_en: No, we've long done restoring the reel, but it isn't with us at the moment...

t_ch: 「钟表匠」没画第五部？
t_en: The Watchmaker didn't create the fifth film?

t_ch: 不，「钟表匠」先生早已完成了这部作品……
t_en: No, Mr. Watchmaker has completed the piece for quite some time already...

t_ch: 但当初我们的影片送审时，鸢尾花家系唯独封禁了这部《美梦大机密》的胶卷——他们非说影片内容有问题，必须得到「钟表匠」先生的出面解释。
t_en: But when we submitted the series for review, the Iris Family only banned Dreamjoy Secrecy. They insisted that there were issues with the content and demanded an explanation from Mr. Watchmaker.

t_ch: 真是可恶…他们肯定是知道「钟表匠」先生现在没法露面，故意提出这样的要求来为难我们！
t_en: It's a dirty move... I'm sure they're deliberately making things difficult for us knowing Mr. Watchmaker isn't around.

t_ch: 导演，听说来客说您是家族的贵客，不知能否由您出面，让家族给个面子？
t_en: Director, since you're an important guest of The Family, do you mind stepping in to convince them to take it easy on us?

t_ch: 没问题。
t_en: No problem.

t_ch: 我试试吧…
t_en: I'll try...

t_ch: 那必须给我面子！
t_en: I'm sure they'll listen to me!

t_ch: 对吧，我相信您一定能让他们让步的！
t_en: Absolutely. I'm sure you can convince them!

t_ch: 太感谢了！鸢尾花家系的审查工作就在<color=#dbc291ff>匹诺康尼大剧院</color>进行，就麻烦您走一趟了，期待您的好消息！
t_en: Thank you so much! The Iris Family conducts the review at the <color=#dbc291ff>Penacony Grand Theater</color>. It'd be great if you could make a trip there. I'll be waiting for your good news!

t_ch: 导演，鸢尾花家系的电影审查工作在<color=#dbc291ff>匹诺康尼大剧院</color>进行，封禁的作品应该都保存在那里。
t_en: Director, the Iris Family conducts the movie review at the <color=#dbc291ff>Penacony Grand Theater</color>. The reels of banned movies are kept there.

t_ch: 您是家族的贵客，若是有空，想请您让家族给个面子，归还《美梦大机密》的胶卷——当然，希望这事不干扰您的剪辑。
t_en: You're an important guest of The Family. If you have the time, I wonder whether you can ask The Family for a favor and return Dreamjoy Secrecy's film reel — Of course, provided this doesn't interfere with your editing job.

t_ch: 片名：《盛会终焉》
t_en: Title: Gala of Finality

t_ch: 该片为葛瑞迪影业制作的灾难片，描绘了匹诺康尼的末日——因为苜蓿草家系的疏忽，纸醉金迷的社会在经济危机下崩溃，贪心的人们引来了可怕的超级忆域迷因。
t_en: The End of the Gala is a disaster film produced by Grady Films, depicting the apocalypse of Penacony. Due to the negligence of the Alfalfa Family, the society that is obsessed with extravagance collapses during the economic crisis. The greed of the people attracts the much-dreaded super Memory Zone Memes.

t_ch: 无所作为的家族与美梦一同覆灭，匹诺康尼最终变为了无人造访的宇宙贫民窟……
t_en: The incompetent Family perishes along with the dream, leaving Penacony a desolate slum in the universe, visited by no one...

t_ch: 审核批注：危言耸听，有损家族的对外形象，建议禁止公映。
t_en: Review: An attempt at scaremongering that can damage the image of The Family. The movie should be banned.

t_ch: 片名：《闹钟小崽子》
t_en: Title: Alarm Clockie

t_ch: 该片为逐梦客制作的独立动画，讲述了「闹钟小崽子」在「瞌睡小镇」，与「努努哥们儿」与「纸壳子小鸟」一起冒险，挑战邪恶的「石疙瘩总裁」的故事。
t_en: Alarm Clockie is an independent film produced by a Dreamchaser. The story is about Alarm Clock Boy's adventure in Sleepy Town alongside Brother Nunoo, Cardboard Birds, and their challenge against President Pebble.

t_ch: 审核批注：影片内容与形象设计侵犯了克劳克影业相关权益，在「钟表小子」形象版权逾期前禁止公映。
t_en: Review: The story and character design of the movie violated the rights and interests of Clock Studios. The movie is banned until the expiry of Clockie's copyright.

t_ch: 片名：《旧时代存护战士》
t_en: Title: Aeon Genesis Preservation

t_ch: 该片为星际和平娱乐制作的引进作品，以「第二次帝皇战争」之后的宇宙为舞台，讲述了星际和平公司的年轻员工们操纵机甲，以琥珀王之名捍卫宇宙和平的故事。
t_en: This is a foreign film imported and produced by Interastral Peace Entertainment. The story is set in the universe after the occurrence of the Emperor's War II and centers around young IPC employees who pilot mechas to maintain galactic peace in the name of the Amber Lord.

t_ch: 审核批注：<unbreak>23</unbreak>至<unbreak>53</unbreak>分钟片段，公司机甲对战的怪物「百臂的原罪」疑似影射某位「调弦师」。以及片中含有大量植入广告与「存护」信仰宣传，建议删减后上映。
t_en: Review: From the <unbreak>23</unbreak>rd to the <unbreak>53</unbreak>rd minute, the monster named "Inherent Sin of Hundred Arms" that the IPC mechas are up against appears to allude to a certain Chordmaster. Additionally, the movie contains a lot of advertisements and elements that promote the belief in Preservation. It is recommended to cut the violating scenes before releasing the movie.

t_ch: 片名：《美梦往事5：美梦大机密》
t_en: Title: Dreamjoy Memoir 5: Dreamjoy Secrecy

t_ch: 该片为《美梦往事》的第五部，讲述了美梦小镇在繁荣过后，■■■爷与钟表■■分道扬镳。为了守护小镇的■■与■■，钟表■■建立了■■村，开始了■■■■■……
t_en: This movie is the fifth installment of the Dreamjoy Memoir series, which tells the story of Dreamville after its prosperity, where Clock■■ and Old ■■■ ■■■■ part ways. To preserve the ■■■■■■■ and ■■■■■ of the town, Clock■■ sets up ■■■■■ville and begins ■■■ ■■■■■■■■ ■■■■■■...

t_ch: 审核批注：<s>包含敏感信息，建议封禁。</s>不符合《钟表小子》作品的过往风格，可能造成不良影响。建议暂时封禁，需传唤主创人员进行面谈。
t_en: Review: This movie contains controversial topics and should be banned. The style of this movie deviates from the previous Clockie pieces and has the potential to cause negative effects. A temporary ban is recommended, and an interview should be arranged with its creator.

t_ch: 不许动，你怎么跑到这里来的！现在匹诺康尼大剧院禁止对外开放……
t_en: Halt! Who goes there? The Penacony Grand Theater is currently closed to the public...

t_ch: 等等，你看起来有点眼熟啊…哦！我之前在克劳克影视乐园见过你！
t_en: Wait, you look kinda familiar... Oh! I met you at the Clock Studios Theme Park before!

t_ch: 你还真是爱跑到奇怪的地方游览呢——如果不想被猎犬带走，就请自报一下身份吧。
t_en: You really have a thing for odd places, huh? Tell me who you are or I'll have you nabbed by the Bloodhounds.

t_ch: 我是星穹列车的无名客。
t_en: I'm one of the Nameless on the Astral Express.

t_ch: 我是匹诺康尼的英雄。
t_en: I'm the hero of Penacony.

t_ch: 我是传说中的银河球棒侠。
t_en: I'm the legendary Galactic Baseballer.

t_ch: 嗯，这个名号…我想起来了，听说最近帮助家族解决「麻烦」的是一位灰发的贵宾，应该就是你吧？
t_en: Hmm, that title... You're the gray-haired guest who has recently helped The Family with their trouble, aren't you?

t_ch: 失敬失敬，容我重新自我介绍一下——我是鸢尾花家系的成员米莫萨，除了商务沟通工作，还负责匹诺康尼影视作品的管理工作。
t_en: Please forgive my rudeness earlier and allow me to introduce myself again. I'm Mimosa, a member of the Iris Family. Aside from business liaisons, I'm also in charge of managing the film and television productions in Penacony.

t_ch: 您跑到被封锁的大剧院中，是有什么诉求吗？
t_en: Is there something you're looking for in the barred theater?

t_ch: 我是来找动画胶卷的。
t_en: I'm looking for an animation film reel.

t_ch: 动画胶卷…哦，你说的一定是克劳克影业的《美梦大机密》。
t_en: Animation... Oh, you must be talking about Dreamjoy Secrecy by Clock Studios.

t_ch: 呵呵，无名客还真是像传闻中的一样乐于助人，克劳克影业最近的四部电影都是你帮忙制作的吧？
t_en: Heh, the Nameless are indeed as helpful as they are described! You helped with the production of the four films recently released by Clock Studios, didn't you?

t_ch: 但非常抱歉，我不能把最后一部影片的胶卷给你——这部影片是由梅芙恩家主亲自封禁的，它的内容不符合家族制定的规范，需要导演「钟表匠」出面解释。
t_en: I'm very sorry, but I can't give you the reel of this film. The decision to ban it was made by Maeven herself because its content didn't comply with The Family's standards. Its director, the Watchmaker, needs to come forward with an explanation.

t_ch: 他来不了。
t_en: He can't make it here.

t_ch: 是啊，「钟表匠」来不了…那非常抱歉，这部影片也没办法上映。
t_en: Indeed, the Watchmaker can't make it here... In that case, I'm sorry to say that the release of the film is forbidden.

t_ch: 这部影片的导演是我。
t_en: I'm the director.

t_ch: 不…这部影片的导演是「钟表匠」，也只能是「钟表匠」。
t_en: No... The director of this film is the Watchmaker. It can't possibly be anyone else.

t_ch: 我就是「钟表匠」。
t_en: I'm the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 呵呵，您不必开这种玩笑，我很清楚「钟表匠」的身份。
t_en: Haha, that's quite a sad joke. I'm well aware of whom the Watchmaker is.

t_ch: 「钟表小子」系列动画发展至今，早已不是「钟表匠」一个人的作品了。它承载了匹诺康尼的精神，承载了美梦的形象——欢欣、美好、和谐……
t_en: The Clockie series has evolved to become something beyond a mere piece of work by the Watchmaker. It embodies the spirit of Penacony and represents the image of a dream — joyful, pleasant, and harmonious...

t_ch: 但它的创作者却不断往里面注入自己的观点与思想，特别是这部影片中…居然加入了这么多对现实的影射，简直是在破坏钟表小子在人们心中的形象……
t_en: However, its creator keeps incorporating his own views and ideas into his works, especially in this film... There are simply too many references to elements in the real world that it is ruining Clockie's image...

t_ch: 说出这些话的时候，米莫萨眉头紧皱，仿佛那些话触碰到了她心中的某些伤口。
t_en: Mimosa's brows furrow as she speaks as though those words are triggering a wound inside her.

t_ch: 你认识「钟表匠」？
t_en: Do you know the Watchmaker personally?

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 没什么，请你先离开吧，我还有很多工作需要处理。
t_en: It's nothing. If you don't mind, I'd like to get back to my work. Please just leave.

t_ch: 米莫萨不再理会你，她看着柜中的胶卷，陷入了沉思。
t_en: Mimosa no longer pays attention to you. She stares at the film reels in the cabinet, lost in thought.

t_ch: （压抑的心情、恍惚的神态、矛盾的言语…）
t_en: (Suppressed emotions, dazed expression, conflicted language...)

t_ch: （感觉她似乎隐藏了什么事情，尝试走进她的内心看看吧……）
t_en: (I can tell she is hiding something. Let's find out what's on her mind...)

t_ch: 贵客，你怎么还在这里？就算你是家族的恩人，我也不能让你一直干扰我的工作。
t_en: Why are you still sticking around? Even though you've done The Family a favor, I can't allow you to keep interrupting my work.

t_ch: 尝试洞察她的内心。
t_en: Try reading her inner thoughts.

t_ch: 打扰了。
t_en: Sorry.

t_ch: 没关系…请快点离开吧。
t_en: Never mind... Just take your leave soon.

t_ch: 米莫萨似乎恢复了镇静的神态，但她的眼中依然带着不安的波澜。
t_en: Mimosa seems to have regained her composure, but there is still a hint of unrest in her eyes.

t_ch: 「钟表匠」…唉……
t_en: Watchmaker... Alas...

t_ch: 她低语着那个你所熟悉的名号，但在这样的情绪之中，她似乎无法对你打开心房……
t_en: She whispers the familiar name. It seems unlikely that she will open up to you when getting caught up in emotions.

t_ch: 米莫萨的放松了皱紧的眉头，露出了无奈的微笑。
t_en: Mimosa relaxes her furrowed brows and smiles in resignation.

t_ch: 说实话，他还真是了不起——哪怕是这么糟糕的题材，看上去还是那么有趣。
t_en: Honestly speaking, he's truly amazing. Despite the terrible subject matter, the story still looks pretty interesting.

t_ch: 但钟表小子就应该是一部单纯的动画才对，不宣讲任何道理，不承载任何意义。
t_en: However, Clockie should just be an animation, not a carrier of propaganda or underlying messages.

t_ch: 贵客，你难道不这么觉得吗？
t_en: Don't you think so?

t_ch: 确实，看个乐子。
t_en: Indeed, people should have fun watching it.

t_ch: 没错，这种动画最吸引人的东西就是趣味，至于其他东西，观众不想看到，家族更不想看到。
t_en: Exactly. The main selling point of this genre is fun. As for other stuff, neither the viewers nor The Family are interested.

t_ch: 不，它是可以有内涵的。
t_en: No, it can be meaningful too.

t_ch: 可以有，但没必要。这种动画最吸引人的东西就是趣味，至于其他东西，观众不想看到，家族更不想看到。
t_en: It can, but it isn't necessary. The main selling point of this genre is fun. As for other stuff, neither the viewers nor The Family are interested.

t_ch: 观看钟表小子的影迷成千上万，又有几人能看出它趣味之下的内涵与隐喻，又能有几人是为了这些东西才进入影院的呢？
t_en: Clockie has tons of fans, but how many of them can perceive the underlying messages and metaphors under the fun façade? And how many of them go to the cinemas for those things?

t_ch: 呵呵，钟表小子决不能承担那么多沉重的东西——他的故事就应该像糖果一样，没有酸涩，只有甜蜜——
t_en: Hehehe, Clockie must not bear so many heavy themes — His story should be like candies. There's no bitterness, only sweetness —

t_ch: 米莫萨笑着递来钟表小子主题的软糖，但你感到她的笑容有些沉重，就像这包零食的口味一样复杂。
t_en: Mimosa smiles and hands you some Clockie-themed soft candies, but you feel her smile looks rather heavy, as complicated and mixed as the tastes of this bag of snacks.

t_ch: 米莫萨面露复杂的微笑，看着柜中的胶卷若有所思。
t_en: With a smile that conveys complicated emotions, Mimosa casts a contemplative gaze at the film reel in the cabinet.

t_ch: 尝试再次洞察她的内心。
t_en: Try reading her thoughts again.

t_ch: 打扰了。
t_en: Sorry.

t_ch: 她依旧保持着那副笑容，一言不发。
t_en: The smile remains on her face, but she doesn't utter a word.

t_ch: 米莫萨攥紧拳头，颤抖了起来。
t_en: Mimosa clenches her fists and starts shaking.

t_ch: 这个家伙，真是太自私了，到底要给我们的美梦添堵到什么时候！
t_en: That guy's too selfish. When is he going to stop ruining the Dreamscape!?

t_ch: 他最大的心愿就是把匹诺康尼搞得一团糟——这么没有同理心，还希望别人通过作品来理解他，真是荒谬！
t_en: Turning Penacony into a chaotic mess is his biggest wish. How can he expect others to understand him through his works when he has zero empathy!?

t_ch: 贵宾，你应该听说过这位「钟表匠」的事迹吧——扰乱梦境的治安，制造混乱，用谎言把大家骗的团团转…这样的人，值得你为他付出吗？
t_en: You must have heard about Watchmaker's deeds, right? He disturbed the peace of Penacony, created confusion, and took everyone for a ride with his lies... Is a man like him worthy of your support?

t_ch: 你并不了解他。
t_en: You don't know him well enough.

t_ch: 呵，我比你了解他，我比所有人都了解他！
t_en: Ha, I know him better than you and everyone else!

t_ch: 我认为值得。
t_en: I think he is worthy of my support.

t_ch: 好啊，那你就继续给他卖命吧，等到发现自己被骗之后千万别后悔！
t_en: Well, you can keep working for him. Don't regret it when you realize you've been duped!

t_ch: 有点道理。
t_en: You've got a point.

t_ch: 呵，你比那些没脑子的逐梦客要聪明多了，这种家伙不值得追随！
t_en: Heh, you're a lot smarter than those dumb Dreamchasers. That kind of guy isn't worth following!

t_ch: 想想看，当年他用印着钟表小子的金纸片把「烫金的时刻」弄得鸡飞狗跳，现在又要用钟表小子的动画制造混乱——这种人会在乎自己的追随者吗？会在乎钟表小子的影迷吗？
t_en: Remember years ago when he used golden papers with Clockie's image printed on them and made a mess of Gilded Hour? Now, he's wreaking havoc again with Clockie's animation. Would such a person care about his followers? Would he care about Clockie's fans?

t_ch: 米莫萨丢给你一张金表钞，上面的钟表小子笑脸盈盈，但米莫萨却怒容满面。
t_en: Mimosa passes you a Clock Credit note. The Clockie's portrait on it is all smiles, but Mimosa is furious.

t_ch: 米莫萨面露怒意，攥紧了双拳，死死盯着柜中的胶卷。
t_en: Looking livid, Mimosa clenches her fists tightly and fixes her unflinching gaze on the film reel in the cabinet.

t_ch: 尝试再次洞察她的内心。
t_en: Try reading her thoughts again.

t_ch: 打扰了。
t_en: Sorry.

t_ch: 她的思绪依然在怒火中燃烧，没有作出回应。
t_en: Consumed by rage, she doesn't respond.

t_ch: 米莫萨面露忧伤，拿出了柜中的胶卷。
t_en: Looking disheartened, Mimosa retrieves the film reel from the cabinet.

t_ch: 这么多年了…他居然还在坚持最初的决定，真是个老顽固……
t_en: After all these years... he still stands by his decision right from the start. What a stubborn man...

t_ch: 你还好吧？
t_en: Are you alright?

t_ch: 听上去有故事啊。
t_en: Sounds like you have a story to tell.

t_ch: 你说的「他」是谁？
t_en: Who's "he"?

t_ch: 恕我冒犯，看到你不着调的样子，我就想起了一些关于他的事——关于「钟表匠」的事。
t_en: No offense, but the way you wander around aimlessly reminds me of him, the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 那些回忆本该被遗忘，但既然想起来了，不妨和你说说吧，毕竟他也是你们「无名客」的前辈。
t_en: Those memories are supposed to fade away, but since they've now come to mind, I might as well share them with you. After all, he's your Nameless senior.

t_ch: 你可能不会相信——我曾经也参与过钟表小子的创作，和克劳克影业的员工不同，我在一个…神秘的地方，和「钟表匠」一起绘制原稿。
t_en: Believe it or not, I was once involved in the creation of Clockie. Unlike other employees of Clock Studios, I drew the sketches at a secret place together with the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 他亲口对我说过，他希望钟表小子为人们带来快乐和希望的作品…但这些都是谎言，那家伙总是在影片里加入一些莫名其妙的情节，也不愿跟我解释灵感的来源。
t_en: He told me he wanted Clockie to be a work that brought joy and hope to people... but it was a lie. That guy had a habit of adding unreasonable plots into the animation and didn't bother to explain how he came about those ideas.

t_ch: 我带着困惑跟随他创作，付出了无数心血。但当我想要为作品增加新的创意时，他又会极力反对——他不容许任何人干预自己的创作，简直就是个自私的暴君！
t_en: Despite my confusion, I worked for him and poured my heart and soul into my work. Every time I proposed new ideas for the project, he'd vehemently object to them — he didn't want anyone to interfere with his creation. He's just a selfish tyrant!

t_ch: 也正因如此，我才离开了他，在匹诺康尼寻找更纯粹的艺术，希望能为美梦带来真正的快乐，所以才加入了鸢尾花家系。
t_en: I decided to leave him because of that. I wanted to look for a purer form of art that could bring true happiness to Penacony, so I joined the Iris Family.

t_ch: 但我依然很重视钟表小子…如今我向家族提议收购版权，也是为了保护它——如果克劳克影业创作的内容继续冒犯家族，那么可能会面临比现在更糟的结局……
t_en: I still care a lot about Clockie... I suggested that The Family acquire its copyright to protect it... If the content created by Clock Studios continues to violate The Family's standards, Clockie could end up much worse than its current predicament...

t_ch: 家族这么小气？
t_en: Is The Family really that unforgiving?

t_ch: 毕竟美梦是由家族管理的，即便是自由的匹诺康尼，也要遵守家族的底线。
t_en: After all, it's The Family that runs the Dreamscape. Even a place with as much freedom as Penacony has to go by its rules.

t_ch: 家族很讨厌钟表小子吗？
t_en: Does The Family hate Clockie?

t_ch: 倒也不是，家主梅芙恩其实是《钟表小子》的影迷，听说「音符小姐」的原型就是她。
t_en: Not really. Our family head, Maeven, is actually a fan of Clockie. I heard that she is the inspiration behind the "Miss Note" character.

t_ch: 在这部《美梦大机密》中，钟表小子与过去的伙伴发生了冲突，情节也走向了一个并不美好的方向。
t_en: In Dreamjoy Secrecy, Clockie has a fallout with his companions and the plot takes on a dark turn.

t_ch: 对于那些认为钟表小子别有深意的观众来说，这样的内容或许会让观众产生不必要的猜测和臆想。
t_en: For the viewers who believe the animation carries an underlying meaning, this kind of content might encourage unnecessary speculation and conjecture.

t_ch: 「同谐」包容不同观念，但不会包容可能诞育危险的思想——艺术作品的煽动力远比你想的要可怕。
t_en: The Harmony embraces diverse concepts, but not those that can lead to danger. The provocative nature of artistic works is far more formidable than you might imagine.

t_ch: 梅芙恩家主说过：「艺术家和虚构史学家一样危险」。他们不生产真相，而是创造故事，激发情绪；而情绪会造成怎样的后果，没人能够预测。
t_en: Lady Maeven once said, "Artists are as dangerous as History Fictionologists." Their creations aren't about truth. Instead, they are about stirring up emotions, and no one can predict the impact of emotions.

t_ch: 家族不是不讲人情，按照家主的意思，这份胶卷可以解禁，但必须要「钟表匠」本人表态——他到底想用《钟表小子》表达什么？是制造混乱，还是另有所谋？
t_en: The Family isn't being unforgiving. As stated by the family head, the film could be unbanned provided that the Watchmaker could come forward with an explanation. What kind of message is he trying to convey with Clockie? Is he trying to create confusion or does he have some kind of hidden agenda?

t_ch: 可惜，我知道他已经…不在了。我们永远没办法理解他的想法，所以这部胶卷我不能归还予你。
t_en: I know he's... no longer around, and it's a shame. Since we can never understand what he really thinks, I can't let you have the film reel.

t_ch: 也许一切还有转机。
t_en: Maybe there's still a chance to turn things around.

t_ch: 他也许留下了什么。
t_en: Maybe he left something behind.

t_ch: 米莫萨看着手中的胶片，仿佛陷入了久远的回忆。
t_en: Mimosa gazes at the film reel in her hands, looking lost in distant memories.

t_ch: 嗯…你说得对，那家伙肯定不会抛下自己的作品不管。
t_en: Hmm... You're right. That guy surely wouldn't abandon his work.

t_ch: 贵客，我要先回「故乡」寻找这个答案，你也可以一起前来…如果你了解「钟表匠」，肯定知道那是哪里——通过「梦中不可能之事」到达的城镇。
t_en: I have to make a trip back to my homeland to search for the answer, and you are welcome to tag along... If you know the Watchmaker well, you'll surely know where I'm referring to... It's a place we can only access by doing something impossible in Dreamscape.

t_ch: 叛徒！
t_en: Traitor!

t_ch: 你为什么要回来！
t_en: Why did you come back!?

t_ch: 对不起……
t_en: I'm sorry...

t_ch: 你这个流梦礁的叛徒，居然还敢回来！
t_en: How dare you come back, you traitor to Dreamflux Reef!

t_ch: 快滚出流梦礁，滚回你的家族！
t_en: Get out of Dreamflux Reef and go back to your Family!

t_ch: 等等，我是米莫萨啊，是「钟表匠」的……
t_en: Wait, I'm Mimosa. I'm Watchmaker's...

t_ch: 呸，你还有脸提这个名字？「钟表匠」先生把你养大，你居然投奔他的敌人！
t_en: Tsk, can't believe you have the face to mention his name! Mr. Watchmaker raised you, and yet you sided with his enemies!

t_ch: 「钟表匠」先生在晚年还在挂念着你，难道你就不会感到羞愧吗！
t_en: Mr. Watchmaker still thought about you in his twilight years! Don't you feel ashamed of yourself!?

t_ch: 我…我很抱歉……
t_en: I... I'm very sorry...

t_ch: 道歉就够了？我们一定要替「钟表匠」好好教训你这个没良心的东西！
t_en: Do you think an apology is enough? We must teach you ingrate a lesson on the Watchmaker's behalf!

t_ch: 有点过分了。
t_en: That's a little harsh.

t_ch: 有话好好说。
t_en: Please don't resort to violence.

t_ch: 贵客……
t_en: My distinguished guest...

t_ch: 你谁啊？看着不是这儿的人吧？
t_en: Who are you? You don't look like a local.

t_ch: 估计是这个叛徒带进来的走狗，跟着一起挨揍吧！
t_en: Must be an underling of that traitor. Just beat the two of them up!

t_ch: 哎哟，这家伙还挺有本事的……
t_en: Ugh, {M#he}{F#she}'s a tough one...

t_ch: 没事，我刚刚叫来了米凯先生，他们完蛋了！
t_en: Don't worry, I just asked Mr. Micah to be here. They're finished!

t_ch: 米凯先生！家族的叛徒和走狗就交给您了！
t_en: Mr. Micah! We're counting on you to punish the traitor and her underling!

t_ch: 抱歉，星穹列车的朋友，让你见笑了。
t_en: I'm sorry you have to go through this, my friend from Astral Express.

t_ch: 啊，星穹列车…难道是「钟表匠」先生的……
t_en: Ah, Astral Express... Could that be Mr. Watchmaker's...

t_ch: 唉，你们快走吧，别再给流梦礁丢脸了。
t_en: You guys should go. Don't embarrass Dreamflux Reef anymore.

t_ch: 好、好！快走吧，咱们好像惹错人了！
t_en: O—Okay! Let's bolt. I think we've messed with the wrong person!

t_ch: 真对不住，{NICKNAME}，米莫萨，你们没事吧？
t_en: My apologies, {NICKNAME} and Mimosa. Are you guys alright?

t_ch: 我…我没事…
t_en: I... I'm fine...

t_ch: …哥哥。
t_en: ...Brother.

t_ch: 你们是…兄妹？
t_en: You two are... siblings?

t_ch: 你也是钟表匠的孩子？
t_en: Are you also a child of the Watchmaker?

t_ch: 米莫萨和我一样，都是钟表匠收养的孤儿，像我们这样的孩子在流梦礁有很多。
t_en: Just like Mimosa, I'm also an orphan adopted by Watchmaker. There are many more kids like us in Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 长大之后，我留在了流梦礁，而米莫萨选择前往美梦，追求她想要的生活。
t_en: When we got older, I chose to stay here in Dreamflux Reef while Mimosa pursued the life she desired in Penacony.

t_ch: 对了，倒是你们两个，怎么认识的？
t_en: By the way, how did the two of you meet?

t_ch: 因为《美梦往事》……
t_en: Because of Dreamjoy Memoir...

t_ch: 我想起来了，皮斯和我说过，你参与了这部电影的制作。
t_en: Yes, Pisce told me you were involved in the production of this film series.

t_ch: 因为「钟表匠」留下的胶卷……
t_en: Because of the film reels left behind by the Watchmaker...

t_ch: 胶卷…我想起来了，是原本准备拍成《美梦往事》的胶卷吗？
t_en: Film reels... Oh, I think I know what you're saying. Are those for the Dreamjoy Memoir series?

t_ch: 皮斯和我说过，你参与了这部电影的制作。
t_en: Pisce told me you were involved in the production of this film series.

t_ch: 因为一场不愉快的争吵……
t_en: Because of an unpleasant argument...

t_ch: 咦？你们为什么会发生不愉快的事情？
t_en: Huh? What led to the argument?

t_ch: 不会是因为…最近那部钟表小子的电影吧？
t_en: Was it about... the recent Clockie movie?

t_ch: 皮斯和我说过，你参与了它的制作。
t_en: Pisce told me you were involved in its production.

t_ch: 是在拍摄电影的过程中遇到了什么困难吗？米莫萨在流梦礁担任过「钟表匠」的动画助手，非常熟悉钟表小子的故事，如果技术上有困难，也许你们可以交流……
t_en: Did you run into any problems during the production? Mimosa used to work as the Watchmaker's animation assistant here before, and she knows Clockie's story like the back of her hand. If it's a technical glitch, you can discuss it with her...

t_ch: 不…我们遇到的困难不是技术上的，而是理念上的。
t_en: No... The issue is more ideological than technical.

t_ch: 我看过了父亲留在克劳克影业的胶卷，我想知道——他为什么要把<color=#dbc291ff>关于流梦礁的故事</color>拍进动画。
t_en: I've watched the film reels Father left behind at Clock Studios, and I wondered why he included the <color=#dbc291ff>story about Dreamflux Reef</color> in the animation.

t_ch: 如果我能明白「钟表匠」这么做的理由，或许就能说服梅芙恩家主撤销封禁胶卷的决定，毕竟她要的只是一个解释。
t_en: If I can figure out why he did that, I can probably convince Lady Maeven to lift the ban on the movie. After all, all she wants is an explanation.

t_ch: 呵呵，她还是像以前一样任性，如果「钟表匠」听到这个要求，应该会大声嘲笑这位老朋友吧？
t_en: Hehe, Maeven is still as capricious as ever. If the Watchmaker could hear this, I suppose he would laugh at this old friend of his.

t_ch: 这么长时间过去了，你还是放不下心中的包袱吗？
t_en: Seems like you still can't let go of the past even after all this time.

t_ch: 跟我来吧，他还给你留了些东西……
t_en: Come with me. He left you something...

t_ch: （他们往墓园的方向走了，去看看吧……）
t_en: (They are heading toward the graveyard. Let's follow them...)

t_ch: 这是…「钟表匠」留给我的信？
t_en: Are these... letters from the Watchmaker for me?

t_ch: 是啊，因为我找不到你在哪里，所以我就一直把这些信放在这里，等你来看他的时候就能找到……
t_en: Yeah. I couldn't find you, so I kept these letters here, thinking that you might find them when you visit him...

t_ch: 现在，我终于可以物归原主了。
t_en: Now, I can finally return them to you.

t_ch: 一滴、两滴、三滴…眼泪落在了信纸上。米莫萨仿佛忘记了周围还有人在，轻声念出了信的内容——
t_en: One, two, three drops... Tears fell onto the letter paper. At that moment, Mimosa seems to have forgotten the people around her as she softly verbalizes the letter's content...

t_ch: 「我倔强的小马，你在美梦中过得还好吗？什么时候才能看到这封信呢？」
t_en: "My stubborn pony, how's life in the dream? When will you ever see this letter?"

t_ch: 「你一定还在生我的气吧，气我为什么要画那些不知所云的故事，气我为什么不让你自由地创作，气我为什么从来不解释这样做的理由……」
t_en: "I bet you're still mad at me for painting those ridiculous stories, restricting your freedom to create, and yet I never explain my reasons for doing so..."

t_ch: 「你或许不能接受我的说法，但这是我的真心话：我希望保护你，保护你的理想。」
t_en: "Perhaps you'll find it hard to accept my reason, but I truly mean it — I wish to protect you and your aspiration."

t_ch: 「我知道，你自小向往穹顶的美梦，想去见证逐梦客的幸福，想看到宾客们的笑脸，因为你是一个喜欢为别人带去快乐的孩子。」
t_en: "I know. Ever since you were a child, you aspire to go to the idyllic dreams above the dome, to witness the Dreamchasers' happiness, and to see smiles on the guests' faces. That's because you're someone who loves to bring joy to others."

t_ch: 「所以我害怕，我害怕我在作品中留下的故事会玷污这份纯真，我希望你心中永远有那片美梦，我希望你不要怀疑这个世界。」
t_en: "That's why I'm afraid. I'm afraid the stories I wrote in my creations will tarnish this innocence. I hope that there's always a dream in your heart. I hope you'll never be skeptical of this world."

t_ch: 「但我现在想通了，你是个勇敢又善良的孩子，你的初心一定不会轻易被击垮。所以我愿意告诉你这些，告诉你我为什么要创作钟表小子——」
t_en: "But I've figured it out now. You are a courageous and kind child. Your pure intent will not be crushed easily. Hence, I am now willing to be truthful and tell you the reason for creating Clockie..."

t_ch: 「因为我不想让人们遗忘梦中的一切。」
t_en: "It's because I do not want people to forget everything in their dreams."

t_ch: 「监狱的战争、无数的灾难、噩梦的拓荒、美梦的建立…一路走来，我看到梦中的人们逐渐停滞不前，也不再回望。他们说梦外足够残酷，梦中不应再翻阅血泪书写的史书。」
t_en: "From the conflict in the prison to the countless catastrophes, to the expansion of nightmares, to the establishment of sweet dreams... Along the way, I've witnessed the people in the dreams gradually coming to a standstill without looking back. They said the world outside of dreams is cruel, and one should no longer look back at these sorrowful pasts in dreams."

t_ch: 「但美梦必须向前开拓，也必须向后回望——所以我提起了笔，让从小陪我长大的老朋友来保留，保留这些珍贵的『 记忆』。」
t_en: "However, a pleasant dream must always move forward in a trail of blazing flame and look back. And so, I put my pen to paper and rely on my childhood friends to keep these precious memories for me."

t_ch: 「假如未来的某天，一切历史被颂歌覆盖，一切来日被欢笑掩埋，人们再也无法醒来，钟表小子的故事或许能成为唤醒人们的存在……」
t_en: "If sometime in the future when all histories are covered in carols and all coming days are buried in laughter, and people can never wake up again, Clockie might be the wake-up call to their existence..."

t_ch: 「对了，你还记得吗？我曾经偷偷告诉过你，钟表小子的脑袋其实是个罗盘——指向未来的罗盘。」
t_en: "Oh yes, do you still remember? I secretly told you once that the brain of Clockie is actually a compass — a compass that leads one to the future."

t_ch: 「而现在，这个罗盘是你的了。属于我的过去已经终结，请你用他记录属于你们的未来吧。」
t_en: "And now, this compass belongs to you. My past has already ended. Please use him to record your future."

t_ch: 「——永远爱你的拉格沃克•夏尔•米哈伊尔……」
t_en: "Love always, Mikhail Char Legwork..."

t_ch: 真是一位了不起的动画师。
t_en: What an amazing animation artist.

t_ch: 是啊，「钟表匠」是匹诺康尼最好的动画师…永远都是。
t_en: Yes, the Watchmaker is Penacony's best animation artist... and will always be.

t_ch: 真是一位了不起的父亲。
t_en: What an amazing father.

t_ch: 呵呵，他可不是非常完美的父亲…但我们依然爱他，对吧，米莫萨。
t_en: Heh, he's not the perfect father... but we will always love him. Am I right, Mimosa?

t_ch: 真是一位了不起的开拓者。
t_en: What an amazing trailblazer.

t_ch: 是啊，他用人生向前开拓，用作品铭记身后的路……
t_en: You're right. He has been trailblazing forward with his life and engraving his past memories in his creations...

t_ch: 「钟表匠」…你为什么不早点告诉我？
t_en: The Watchmaker... why didn't you let me know earlier?

t_ch: 米莫萨，他其实一直想要保护你，所以才从未对你提起那些不够光鲜的过去。
t_en: Mimosa, he had always wanted to protect you so he chose not to tell you about his less-than-glamorous past.

t_ch: 但是…如果我早点知道，我就不会离开流梦礁，不会抛弃钟表小子……
t_en: But... if I had known about it earlier, I wouldn't have left Dreamflux Reef and abandoned Clockie...

t_ch: 呵呵，用他的话来说，那是你自己选择的「航路」。而且——现在还不晚，不是吗？
t_en: Heh heh. To put it in his words, that is the "route" you've chosen. Moreover... it isn't too late now, is it?

t_ch: …是啊，现在还不晚。
t_en: ...Yes, it's not too late.

t_ch: 我会马上觐见梅芙恩家主，告诉她——「钟表匠」只是想记录匹诺康尼不应被忘却的历史，这样的理由她一定能理解的，毕竟……
t_en: I will request to meet Lady Maeven immediately and tell her... the Watchmaker is only trying to document the history of the Penacony that shouldn't be forgotten. I believe she will understand such a reason. After all...

t_ch: 毕竟？
t_en: After all?

t_ch: 呵呵，没什么，总之她是个讲道理的人，放心吧。
t_en: Heh, it's nothing. She's a reasonable person, so don't worry about it.

t_ch: 米莫萨，你还是和小时候一样聪明，做事雷厉风行，心眼还不少。
t_en: Mimosa, you're still as clever as you were when you were a child, getting things done promptly and decisively, with a keen eye.

t_ch: 行了，以后再叙旧吧，现在最重要的事是让这部影片成功上映。
t_en: All right, we can always catch up later. What's important now is to get this movie successfully released.

t_ch: 贵客，我明白了——钟表小子固然能给人带来快乐，但真正的快乐不是遗忘沉重和悲伤，而是在感受到悲伤之后，依然能够用释然的态度去铭记一切。
t_en: I understand. It is undeniable that Clockie can bring happiness to people. However, real happiness doesn't come from forgetting the sorrowful past, but from calmly embracing the past after experiencing the sorrow.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我一定会想办法把胶片还给克劳克影业的，请一定要让它登上银幕！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I'll think of a way to pass this film reel to the Clock Studios. Please, you must make sure it hits the screen!

t_ch: 你怎么又来了，现在《美梦往事》正在热映，克劳克影业蒸蒸日上，不需要家族来掺一脚！
t_en: Why are you here again? Dreamjoy Memoir is now showing, and the business of the Clock Studios is on the rise. There's no need for The Family to be involved!

t_ch: 真的吗？你们的最后一部作品做完了吗？
t_en: Are you sure? Have you completed the final work?

t_ch: 这个…不就卡在你们家族手里吗！居然想用这个来要挟我们，无耻……
t_en: That... is all because of The Family's interference! To think you can use this method to threaten us... How shameless...

t_ch: 不是的，胶卷给你。
t_en: That's not true. Here, you can have the film reel.

t_ch: 啊？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 你们的排期现在很紧吧？那就别磨蹭了，之后的动画制作我也会帮忙。
t_en: You must be on a tight schedule now, isn't it? Then stop wasting time. I'll also help with the animation later.

t_ch: 这、这又是什么计策，我不会上当的，等导演回来……
t_en: Wh—What are you up to this time? I'm not going to fall into your trap. We'll wait for the director to be back...

t_ch: 啊！导演，原来您已经回来了，这是去哪了，花了这么长时间？
t_en: Ah! Director, you're back! Where did you go? What took you so long?

t_ch: 去找胶卷了。
t_en: I was finding the reel.

t_ch: 难道是您让这位女士来归还胶卷的吗——不愧是匹诺康尼的英雄，面子也太大了！
t_en: Could it be that you've sent this lady to give us the film reel? No doubt you're the hero of Penacony! This is such an honor!

t_ch: 去跟米莫萨谈判了。
t_en: I went to negotiate with Mimosa.

t_ch: 这…您可真厉害，三言两语就能让她亲自来还胶卷！
t_en: Well... You're incredible. Just with a few words from you, and she's personally delivered the film reel to us!

t_ch: 去了「钟表匠」的居所。
t_en: I went to the Watchmaker's house.

t_ch: 天哪，他还好吗？！
t_en: Oh my goodness! Is he doing okay?

t_ch: 我来解释吧，事情是这样的……
t_en: Let me explain. This is what happened...

t_ch: 米莫萨跟皮斯简述了刚刚的经历。
t_en: Mimosa briefly describes to Pisce what she has just experienced.

t_ch: 原来如此，《钟表小子》的背后原来有这么多故事…「钟表匠」先生，你真了不起……
t_en: I see. There are so many stories behind the Clockie... Mr. Watchmaker, you're a great man...

t_ch: 那么——无论出于对观众的尊重，还是为了继承「钟表匠」先生的精神，克劳克影业都会努力将《美梦往事》的最后一部制作完成！
t_en: If that's the case, whether it's out of respect for the audience, or to carry on the spirit of Mr. Watchmaker, Clock Studios promises to do its best and complete his last film of Dreamjoy Memoir!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}导演，还有鸢尾花家系的…米莫萨顾问，接下来的工作就拜托你们了！
t_en: Director {NICKNAME} and... Consultant Mimosa from the Iris Family, I'm counting on you two for the remaining work!

t_ch: 贵客，一起为「钟表匠」最后的作品画上句号吧，您准备好了吗？
t_en: My distinguished guest, let's finish up the Watchmaker's last work together. Are you ready?

t_ch: 导演，最后的胶卷已经准备好了，就等你们来进行最后的剪辑了。
t_en: Director, the last film reel is ready. You can now proceed with the final editing.

t_ch: 开始剪辑。
t_en: Begin editing.

t_ch: 等我一会儿。
t_en: Give me a moment.

t_ch: 你们可要尽快啊，观众们都急坏了！
t_en: You need to hurry. The audience is getting antsy!

t_ch: 《美梦往事》在克劳克影视乐园举办了公映活动，影城中的观众熙熙攘攘……
t_en: The grand public screening of Dreamjoy Memoir takes place amidst the vibrant atmosphere of the Clock Studios Theme Park, which is teeming with eager audiences...

t_ch: 快看，是钟表小子的新电影！
t_en: Look! That's Clockie's latest film!

t_ch: 哎呀，太久没在影城里看动画了，真是令人怀念……
t_en: Oh, it has been such a long time since I watched animations in the theme park. How nostalgic...

t_ch: 导演，快看呐，观影的人都快塞满影城了——身份显赫的宾客、年轻的逐梦客、尊贵的家族成员…他们都是为了这部影片才来的！
t_en: Director, look! The theme park is filled with all kinds of people — distinguished guests, young Dreamchasers, honored members of The Family — and they are all here for the movie!

t_ch: 「钟表匠」要是见到这样的场面，一定会很欣慰的，感谢您的辛苦付出！
t_en: If the Watchmaker were to witness this, he'd be super glad. Thank you so much for your hard work!

t_ch: 对了，不知道「钟表匠」的想法有没有传达给各位观众…导演，您能去问问观众们吗？
t_en: Right. I wonder if the Watchmaker's message managed to get passed to the audience... Director, can you ask the viewers for their opinions?

t_ch: 嘿，小灰毛，这是你拍的动画？挺厉害的嘛！
t_en: Hey, Gray Hair, is this your animation? Not bad!

t_ch: 你觉得这部影片怎么样？
t_en: What do you think of this movie?

t_ch: 这个嘛…我的审美要求可是很高的哦，这场梦境中昼夜不停地上演着无聊的悲剧，喜剧，肥皂剧——还有一堆让人昏昏欲睡的电影。
t_en: Well... I've got high aesthetic standards. This Dreamscape is always showing boring tragedies, typical comedies, mundane soap operas... and a bunch of sleep-inducing films.

t_ch: 不过这部片子还不赖，至少为这个世界贡献了一些不那么无聊的东西，不至于让我在梦中都打瞌睡。
t_en: But I've got to say, this movie is not bad at all. At least it contributes something less boring to this world, and it doesn't make me doze off even in the dream.

t_ch: 但一切「表演」都带有目的，这部影片的故事应该没这么简单吧，小灰毛？
t_en: Then again, every "performance" has its own intention. I'm sure the story of this movie isn't that simple. Am I right, Gray Hair?

t_ch: 这个嘛……
t_en: Well...

t_ch: 你说得对……
t_en: You're right...

t_ch: 其实这部影片是……
t_en: In fact, this movie is...

t_ch: 嘘，你不用告诉我，让我自己猜吧——到时候肯定有各种各样的解读，没人知道哪个是对的，这样才更有趣！
t_en: Shh, don't tell me. Let me make a guess... I believe there are going to be all sorts of interpretations for the movie in due time, and no one will know which is the truth. That'll make things more interesting!

t_ch: 嗯…说不定木头老爷和石头老板才是真正的好人，美梦小镇的幕后黑手是钟表小子才对……
t_en: Hmm... Perhaps the Old Man Wood and Boss Stone are actually the good ones, while the mastermind behind the Dreamville is Clockie...

t_ch: 花火坏笑着思考情节的不同可能——相信用不了多久，就能从其他观众口中听到她的奇妙分析。
t_en: Sparkle gives a malicious chuckle as she thinks of the different possible plots — you believe it won't take long for you to hear her marvelous analysis from the mouths of the other viewers.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，这真是一部很不错的影片，我很喜欢。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, this is a great movie. I like it very much.

t_ch: 你有什么感想？
t_en: What are your thoughts?

t_ch: 呵呵，其实我平时不怎么看动画，比起创作出来的故事，我更欣赏真实的记录。
t_en: Heheh, I normally don't watch animations. I prefer real life accounts over created stories.

t_ch: 但这部片子不一样，我看得出，这些故事之下似乎藏着秘密——是一段不为人知的漫长时光，对吗？
t_en: But this movie is different. I can tell that there are secrets hidden behind these stories — a lengthy account of an untold story, perhaps?

t_ch: 用这样的方式记录过去，还真是有趣，让我想起了一位同样喜欢将记忆留在镜头中的忆者……
t_en: To use such a method to document the past is interesting. It reminds me of a Memokeeper who also loves to record their memories on the camera...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，记忆是留存往日的刻录，也是启示来日的谶语——祝你与「钟表匠」的作品能撬动这场美梦的未来。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, memories are records of the past and prophecies of the future. I hope your and the Watchmaker's work can shift the future of this dream.

t_ch: 野狼睡去，巨木倾圮，钟表重转，惊醒湖中的鳄鱼…过去的故事已经完结，我很期待这部影片的未来。
t_en: The coyote has fallen asleep, the heavy logs have fallen, the clocks have re-winded, startling the crocodiles in the lake awake... The stories of the past have ended. I'm looking forward to the future of this movie.

t_ch: ——黑天鹅静望着屏幕，给出了一句稍显晦涩的影评。
t_en: ...Black Swan stares quietly at the screen and gives you a perplexing critique of the movie.

t_ch: 这不是{NICKNAME}吗，这片儿是你整的？真他宝贝的爱你！
t_en: {NICKNAME}? Is that you? You cooked up this film? Hot diggity fudge, that's some mighty fine work... absolute cinema!

t_ch: 你别说，我以前一直以为这圆盘脑袋是给没断奶的小孩儿看的，没想到做的还真不错。
t_en: Well I'll be hornswoggled, I reckoned them clock-heads were for young 'uns fresh off the teat. Turns out, they sure did a fine job with 'em.

t_ch: 你怎么评价这部影片？
t_en: How would you rate this movie?

t_ch: 爱你，太爱你了！
t_en: Butter my fudge, it's so lit!

t_ch: 啊？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 啥意思？
t_en: What do you mean?

t_ch: 我也爱你。
t_en: I love fudge too.

t_ch: 喵，我的意思是做的好！特别是那个左轮队长，太他宝贝的帅了！
t_en: Hot fudge, what I mean is y'all did mighty fine! Especially that Captain Revolver, durn handsome as a wild stallion!

t_ch: 大导演，我现在就是你的影迷了，以后多整点爱你片子吧！
t_en: Oh, my great director, I'm now a fan of yers. Please make more fudgin' awesome films like this one!

t_ch: 可惜，现实里没几个像左轮队长这样的英雄，石头老板这种欠喵的小可爱倒是不少，为了点儿破石头就糟蹋别人的家园——如果是我，第一幕就会把他的脑袋打开花。
t_en: Shame, ain't many heroes like Captain Revolver 'round these parts. Plenty of muddle-fudgers like Boss Stone wanderin' 'bout causin' trouble. For a nugget of rock, they'd ruin folks' homesteads. If it were me, I'd open his bean right in the first scene.

t_ch: 波提欧似乎很喜欢你的影片，你的故事将一些意志传递给了他，一定也传递给了更多人。
t_en: Boothill seems to appreciate your movie. Your story has given him some of the intended message and will, and you're certain that many viewers have felt it too.

t_ch: 观众们都很开心…看来哪怕看不出背后的深意，这部影片本身也很有趣。
t_en: The audience is pleased... Even if they can't discern the deeper meaning behind it, the movie itself is quite fun.

t_ch: 是啊，对于寻找快乐的观众，动画就只是动画而已。但如果能有人从镜头、表演、故事甚至一句台词中读出深意…那他就能与「钟表匠」的灵魂相逢，聆听那些过去的梦。
t_en: You're right. For viewers who are here to seek entertainment, the movie is just an animation. But if someone can decipher the hidden message behind the camera work, performance, story, or even a single line of dialogue... then they will be able to meet the Watchmaker's soul and listen to the past dreams.

t_ch: 你们感觉如何？
t_en: What do you all think?

t_ch: 贵客…在一帧帧画面流过的时候，我仿佛看到了伏案工作的「钟表匠」——我能看出来，片中的一些故事，是他按照我过去的想法修改的……
t_en: As the movie scenes played before my eyes, I could see the Watchmaker poring over his work before a desk — I could tell that he amended some stories in the movie based on my past suggestions...

t_ch: 我也是看钟表小子长大的，小时候一直想看流梦礁出现在动画里。而如今终于看到了我们的「清醒村」，也算是如愿以偿了。
t_en: I grew up watching Clockie too, and I have always wanted to see Dreamflux Reef in the animations. And today, I'm finally able to see our Rouseville. That's all I wanted.

t_ch: 贵客，感谢你让这些故事重见天日。
t_en: Thank you for letting these stories hit the screen, distinguished guest.

t_ch: 我已经跟梅芙恩家主申请过了——今后我会作为顾问帮助克劳克影业妥善发展，让钟表小子的指针一直转动下去。
t_en: I've requested it from Lady Maeven. From today onward, I will assist Clock Studios in their steady development as a consultant and keep the hands of Clockie turning.

t_ch: 一定会一直转下去的！
t_en: I'm sure it will continue turning!

t_ch: 钟表小子的故事不会结束！
t_en: The story of Clockie will never end!

t_ch: 我也要感谢你，{NICKNAME}。以这部影片为契机，肯定会有越来越多的人重新拾起被遗忘的过去，拾起清醒的火种，然后点亮未来——
t_en: I want to thank you too, {NICKNAME}. With the help of this movie, I'm sure more people will pick up their forgotten past, ignite the flame of wakefulness within them, and brighten their future...

t_ch: 呵呵，如果「钟表匠」能看到那样的未来，他一定会很高兴的。
t_en: Heheh, I'm sure the Watchmaker will be pleased to see such a future.

t_ch: 不过还真是奇怪，「钟表匠」在流梦礁没有留下任何钟表小子未来的手稿，克劳克影业今后可能真的要靠自己创作了。
t_en: But this is odd. The Watchmaker did not leave any manuscripts about Clockie's future in the Dreamflux Reef. It seems like Clock Studios may have to really create their own future.

t_ch: 呵呵，「钟表匠」不都说清楚了吗？钟表小子今后的故事，要由见证未来的我们讲述……
t_en: Heheh, didn't the Watchmaker say so? The story of Clockie's future is to be told by us, who will be witnessing the future...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}导演，观众的感想如何？
t_en: Director {NICKNAME}, what do the viewers think?

t_ch: 大家评价很好。
t_en: They gave it a high rating.

t_ch: 哈哈，那当然，这套影片可谓是《钟表小子》五百年来最精彩的作品了！
t_en: Haha, that's for sure. This movie is arguably the best work of Clockie in the past 500 years.

t_ch: 似乎有人看出了端倪。
t_en: It seems like someone has noticed the hidden message.

t_ch: 是吗…或许「钟表匠」就是希望看到这样的观众越来越多，让他们在动画中收获快乐之外的东西吧。
t_en: Is that so...? Perhaps the Watchmaker is hoping to see more of such viewers so that they can reap the benefits of the animation beyond the joy of it.

t_ch: 导演，和您一起工作的时候，我就像回到了克劳克影业的黄金时代——我与「钟表匠」一起共事，跟着他一起延续钟表小子的冒险。
t_en: Director, working with you is like returning to the golden age of Clock Studios, when I was still working with the Watchmaker and continuing the adventures of Clockie alongside him.

t_ch: 现在想来，我们那时原来是在铭刻匹诺康尼的历史…我理解「钟表匠」先生为什么要隐瞒这一切了，如果大家都抱着这样的压力创作，作品又怎能带来这么多的欢乐？
t_en: Now that I think about it, we were actually engraving the history of Penacony... I understand why Mr. Watchmaker chose to hide it from everyone. After all, how could the creation of this work bring so much joy if everyone was under such pressure to create it?

t_ch: 克劳克影业亦会带着这样的使命继续前行，守望着美梦，直到每个人都拥有「从梦中醒来」的权利。
t_en: Clock Studios will continue to move forward with this mission. It will watch over the dream until everyone has the right to "wake up from the dream."

t_ch: 那么——随着最后一部影片的上映，《美梦往事》的故事也告一段落了，辛苦您了。
t_en: So, with the release of the last movie, the story of Dreamjoy Memoir has also come to an end for now. Thank you for your hard work.

t_ch: 这就完事了？
t_en: That's it?

t_ch: 终于结束了…
t_en: It's finally over...

t_ch: 再来一百部！
t_en: Let's do a hundred more movies!

t_ch: 这个嘛…其实我们还打算将五部《美梦往事》的故事进行结合，制作一部能让观众一直看下去的「超长篇」电影。
t_en: Well... we're actually planning on combining the five stories from Dreamjoy Memoir to create a "super extended version" of the movie for our audience to watch for a long time.

t_ch: 这也是项庞大的工程，如果您有兴趣继续担任导演，随时欢迎您回来参与制作！
t_en: It is also a huge project. If you're interested in continuing as our director, you're always welcome to come back and be part of the production!

t_ch: 对了，我们的影片取得成功后，大名鼎鼎的苏乐达企业希望能用钟表小子的形象为他们代言，为苏乐达拍摄一组广告。
t_en: Oh right. When our movie is a hit, the renowned SoulGlad hopes to use Clockie's image as their spokesperson to help them shoot a set of commercials.

t_ch: 对方点名邀您担任这部广告片的导演，方便的话就去帮帮忙吧，您的「导演技巧」肯定能借此登上更高的境界。
t_en: They've specially invited you to be the director of this commercial. You should go assist if you're free. Your Directorial Skills will surely be elevated to the next level with this project.

t_ch: 那就这样吧，亲爱的导演——我谨代表克劳克影业向您致敬，我们绝不会忘记您为「钟表匠」先生还有《钟表小子》付出的一切努力！
t_en: Now then, dear director — On behalf of Clock Studios, I salute you! We'll never forget all the hard work you put in for Mr. Watchmaker and the Clockie series!

t_ch: 导演，您回来了，要来进行影片剪辑吗？
t_en: Director, you're back! Do you want to do some film editing?

t_ch: 开始剪辑。
t_en: Begin editing.

t_ch: 我想问你点问题。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 好嘞，深入了解才能创造佳作，您尽管问，我知无不言！
t_en: Sure! An in-depth understanding of the subject is essential in creating a masterpiece. Just ask away! I'll tell you everything I know.

t_ch: 《美梦往事》讲的是什么？
t_en: What is Dreamjoy Memoir about?

t_ch: 您应该知道，「美梦小镇」就是钟表小子和他的伙伴们生活的小镇，这部系列影片就是关于美梦小镇的故事。
t_en: The series revolves around the story of Dreamville, where Clockie and his friends live — I bet you already know this.

t_ch: 钟表小子来到美梦小镇、与镇民们共甘共苦、帮助美梦小镇发展壮大…这一系列故事都会被涵盖在这套影片之中，可以说是《钟表小子》至今以来的故事总集。
t_en: The series will delve into Clockie's arrival in Dreamville, his experiences of sharing life's ups and downs with the townspeople, and his contributions to the town's growth and development... It can be seen as a comprehensive collection of Clockie's story from the very beginning.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼是怎么制作动画的？
t_en: How is animation production done in Penacony?

t_ch: 在匹诺康尼，制作动画的方式非常简单——除了智能绘制、思维显像、算法生成这种常见方法，梦中还有擅长绘画的美梦剧团，可以还原古老的手绘动画质感……
t_en: Producing animations in Penacony is pretty simple. Apart from the common techniques like smart illustration, mental projection, and algorithmic generation, there's also the Sweet Dreams Troupe which is skilled in drawing and capable of recreating the vintage hand-drawn graphic style...

t_ch: 但在克劳克影业，我们的动画分镜都是「钟表匠」先生手工绘制，他也要求我们的动画师亲手创作每一帧的内容。
t_en: But here at Clock Studios, our storyboards are all hand-drawn by Mr. Watchmaker. He insists that our animators create the content for each frame themselves by hand.

t_ch: 现在宇宙中的动画技术越来越高超，画面也越来越精细。但那些作品还是无法在梦中打败手工绘制的《钟表小子》，这也是我们剧组的骄傲！
t_en: As animation technology in the universe continues to advance, the graphics of films nowadays become increasingly sophisticated. Still, none of those works can rival the hand-drawn Clockie in Penacony. We're proud of that!

t_ch: 关于影城遭到的破坏。
t_en: About the destruction of the theme park.

t_ch: 唉，您一说这个我就郁闷，本来影城就是我们最重要的线下收入之一，但前些日子突然被猎犬家系封闭，说是要安全检查……
t_en: Oh, the mere mention of it saddens me. The theme park has always been one of our most important sources of offline revenue, but it was unexpectedly shut down by the Bloodhound Family for what they called a safety inspection...

t_ch: 结果他们检查完之后，我们观影区的大屏幕就被劈成了两半，美梦剧团也都变成了惊梦剧团。猎犬家系还推脱责任，说这是什么「外来者造成的破坏」，真是荒谬！
t_en: Guess what happened after their inspection? The screen in the theater was split in half, and the Sweet Dreams Troupe were all turned into Dreamjolt Troupe. They even tried talking their way out of it by saying the destruction was the doing of some outsiders. How ridiculous!

t_ch: 这还没完，之后隐夜鸫家系的人跑过来，说这里受到的破坏并非寻常，是什么…「虚无」的力量？反正拽了一堆名词，开了一个我们无法接受的修理费。
t_en: That's not all. Later, a bunch of Nightingale Family members showed up, asserting that the destruction was unusual and exhibited signs of some... power of Nihility? Anyway, they inundated us with a barrage of technical terms and demanded an exorbitant repair fee that we simply couldn't accept.

t_ch: 不久之后，鸢尾花家系的人就来聊版权收购——要我看啊，这就是家族内部串通好的，就是想打压我们克劳克影业，夺走钟表小子！
t_en: Shortly after, the Iris Family representative approached us and proposed to acquire our copyrights. If you ask me, those lineages are probably in cahoots with each other to take advantage of us when we're down and claim Clockie as their own!

t_ch: 关于你对「钟表匠」的印象。
t_en: About your impression of the Watchmaker.

t_ch: 这个嘛…我在克劳克影业干了大半辈子，但其实也只见过董事长一面——
t_en: Well... Although I've worked at Clock Studios for most of my life, I've only met the chairman once...

t_ch: 那是我在克劳克影业的升职面谈，他居然把面谈地点放在了「夕阳号」的游轮舞会上。
t_en: It was an interview for my promotion. He actually held the interview at a party on the cruise ship "The Sunset."

t_ch: 我当时喝得醉醺醺的，被人拽到了深处的客房，在那里看到一个头戴礼帽、身着华服的绅士坐在窗边，遥望绚烂的梦泡之海。
t_en: I already had a little too much to drink when I was dragged to a guest room deep in the corridor, inside of which was an immaculately dressed gentleman wearing a hat. He sat next to a window, staring into the distance at the colorful ocean of dream bubbles.

t_ch: 他问我：「你认为《钟表小子》能带给人们什么」，我回答：「幸福、欢笑、感动，以及对美梦的一点儿反思」——但说实话，最后一个是我喝醉了瞎掰的。
t_en: He asked, "In your opinion, what does Clockie bring to the people?" I replied, "Happiness, laughter, heartwarming moments, and a bit of reflection on the sweet dreams." Honestly, the last one was just me blabbering away in my state at the time.

t_ch: 他听到这个答案过后笑了笑，就让我离开了房间。第二天，我就收到了总制片人的任命通知…真是个神秘的人啊。
t_en: He chuckled at my answer before dismissing me from the room. The following day, I received the appointment notice from the executive producer... What a mysterious guy.

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 下次再说。
t_en: Maybe next time.

t_ch: 没问题，随时欢迎您回来参与制作！
t_en: No problem. You're always welcome to come back and be part of the production!

t_ch: 道具损耗、用人成本、拍摄经费…对了，还得算上工期……
t_en: Item wear and tear, manpower costs, filming expenses... Oh right, also gotta factor in the duration...

t_ch: 算下来一共花费…不行，预算超了，哪里还能调整一下……
t_en: So the sum of expenses is... No no, we've exceeded our budget. Where can I shift things around...

t_ch: 皮斯让我来找拍摄广告。
t_en: Pisce asked me to film some adverts here.

t_ch: 稍等片刻，我正在计算此次合作拍摄的总成本。
t_en: Hold on a sec. I'm calculating the total expenses for this filming collab.

t_ch: 久等了，我是十二时刻商业街的营销代理人，此次前来与您沟通广告合作一事。
t_en: Sorry to make you wait. I'm the agent for the Twelve Hours' business street. I've come to speak to you about working on a commercial together.

t_ch: 您就是《美梦往事》的导演吧？事不宜迟，请先看看这次广告的脚本——
t_en: You're the director of Dreamjoy Memoir? Just in time. Please take a look at the script for this commercial...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>《哈努兄弟大劫案》</color>。故事仍以美梦小镇为背景，主要以我方品牌形象与《美梦往事》中的「哈努兄弟」同框出现，互惠共赢。
t_en: Brother Hanu's Great Heist. The story is set in Dreamville, and the main thing is to have our brand's imagery and Brother Hanu from Dreamjoy Memoir be in the same shot. It's a win-win for both sides.

t_ch: 目前各方已准备就位，您准备好就能开机了。
t_en: Everyone's at their marks now. You can start filming whenever you're ready.

t_ch: 没问题，开拍！
t_en: No problem. Action!

t_ch: 等我再琢磨一下…
t_en: Allow me to ponder it a bit more...

t_ch: 目前各方已准备就位。您准备好就可以开机了。
t_en: Everyone's at their marks now. You can start filming whenever you're ready.

t_ch: 没问题，开拍！
t_en: No problem. Action!

t_ch: 等我再琢磨一下…
t_en: Allow me to ponder it a bit more...
